author: Maria Delgado, RN / Telemetry tech J. Boyd; reviewed by Dr. Steven Marsh (Cardiology)
content:
TELEMETRY EVENT DOCUMENTATION — POD#4

Reason for note: Document a sustained atrial fibrillation episode captured on telemetry today.

DETAILED CHARACTERIZATION:
- Onset: approximately 14:30. Telemetry alarmed for an irregular tachyarrhythmia.
- Rhythm: ATRIAL FIBRILLATION (irregularly irregular, no organized P waves). Confirmed by 12-lead ECG obtained at 14:35 (see IMG004; Cardiology over-read by Dr. Marsh).
- Ventricular response: ranged 70 to 105 bpm during the episode (mean ~92). No rapid ventricular response above ~105.
- Symptoms: NONE. Patient was sitting up eating lunch, denied palpitations, chest pain, dyspnea, or lightheadedness throughout.
- Hemodynamics: blood pressure UNAFFECTED (132/78 during the episode); no hypotension.
- Duration: approximately TWO HOURS.
- Resolution: SPONTANEOUS conversion to normal sinus rhythm at ~16:40, WITHOUT any rate-control or rhythm-control medication and WITHOUT electrical cardioversion. Repeat rhythm strip confirmed sinus (rate 78).

This is the second discrete documented occurrence of atrial fibrillation this admission (after the brief self-limited runs on POD#1, see NOTE004). Findings communicated to the Medicine team for management decisions prior to the planned discharge.
attestation: Electronically documented by Maria Delgado, RN; rhythm reviewed with J. Boyd (monitor tech) and Dr. Steven Marsh (Cardiology). 6/14/2027.
